name: Open Studio: First Sunday Drop-In
description: Start your month with a relaxed afternoon in the 2nd floor studios at Heart of Ellsworth. Bring your own project or explore the tools and materials available on-site. All skill levels and media welcome.

name: Gelatin Printing: Spring Botanicals
note: Ages 15+. All materials provided.
description: Karin Otto leads this hands-on gelatin printing workshop focused on early spring botanical forms. Learn layering techniques, ghost printing, and color mixing using real plant materials on the gelatin plate. All materials included.

name: Open Studio Night
description: Drop in to the makerspace for a midweek creative session. Work tables, easels, and basic supplies are available on a first-come basis. A great chance to meet other local makers and get inspired.

name: Community Meeting: Downtown Revitalization Update
address: Ellsworth City Hall, 1 City Hall Plaza, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Light refreshments provided. Public comment period included.
description: The City of Ellsworth hosts an update on the downtown revitalization plan, including streetscape improvements and new public art installations. Heart of Ellsworth staff will present on the makerspace expansion. All residents welcome.

name: First Friday Art Walk
note: Maps available at 16 State Street.
description: Kick off First Friday with a gallery opening at Heart of Ellsworth featuring work by resident studio artists. The 2nd floor galleries showcase new paintings, prints, and mixed media. Light refreshments and live acoustic music.

name: Live Music Friday at Airline Brewing
address: Airline Brewing Co. Pub, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
description: Local Downeast musicians perform a mix of folk, bluegrass, and Americana at Airline Brewing Co. Grab a craft beer and settle in for an evening of live music. Part of the First Friday downtown celebration.

name: Downtown Volunteer Cleanup: Spring Kickoff
address: Franklin Street Parklet, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Meet at Franklin Street Parklet. Gloves and bags provided. Coffee and donuts at check-in.
description: Help get downtown Ellsworth ready for spring. Volunteers tackle litter pickup, bench cleaning, and flower bed prep along Main Street and the Union River walkway. All supplies provided, plus coffee and donuts to fuel the morning.

name: Kids Workshop: Paperbag Books & Journals
note: Ages 8+. Free admission; suggested $10 donation. All materials provided.
description: Kids learn to fold and bind their own journals from paper bags and recycled materials in this fun, hands-on morning workshop. Each child takes home a unique handmade book. Suggested $10 donation supports future free programming.

name: Stick & Stitch Embroidery Workshop
note: Beginner-friendly. All materials included.
description: Colleen Ross teaches the popular stick-and-stitch method where you apply water-soluble patterns to fabric and embroider over them. Perfect for beginners who want beautiful results on their first try. Take home your finished piece and extra patterns.

name: Open Studio Night
description: Wednesday open studio continues with a welcoming space for artists of all media. Bring your own project or experiment with the tools available in the makerspace. Studio monitors on hand to help with equipment.

name: Historic Walking Tour: Mill Town Ellsworth
address: Franklin Street Parklet, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Meet at the Franklin Street Parklet. Wear comfortable shoes. About 1 mile walk.
description: A guided walking tour exploring the history of Ellsworth as a lumber and mill town along the Union River. Led by a local history consultant, the walk covers key sites from the waterfront to Main Street. Free and open to all.

name: Gelatin Printing: Layer & Texture
note: Ages 15+. No experience needed. All materials provided.
description: Explore layering and texture techniques with the gelatin plate in this workshop with Karin Otto. Build complex compositions using stencils, found objects, and brayers. Great for both beginners and returning students.

name: Maker Meetup: Project Share Night
description: Bring a finished project, a work in progress, or just your curiosity to this casual monthly gathering. Short show-and-tell presentations followed by open conversation and networking. All makers welcome, from woodworkers to fiber artists to coders.

name: Live Music at Finn's: St. Patrick's Eve Session
address: Finn's Irish Public House, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
description: Get an early start on St. Patrick's Day with a traditional Irish music session at Finn's. Local musicians lead a lively evening of jigs, reels, and sing-alongs. Full pub menu and drink specials all night.

name: Art Show: Downeast Light — Group Exhibition
note: Exhibition runs through March 31.
description: Opening day for Downeast Light, a group exhibition featuring paintings, photography, and prints by twelve Hancock County artists exploring the unique quality of Maine coastal light. Gallery open daily through end of month.

name: Intro to Papermaking with Plants
note: All materials provided. Wear clothes that can get wet.
description: Karen Olson teaches the ancient craft of making paper by hand using plant fibers gathered from the Maine landscape. Learn to process fibers, form sheets on a mould and deckle, and press your own handmade paper to take home.

name: St. Patrick's Day Pub Crawl
address: Finn's Irish Public House, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: 21+ event. Pick up your passport at Finn's starting at 5 PM.
description: Celebrate St. Patrick's Day with a self-guided pub crawl through downtown Ellsworth. Pick up a passport at Finn's and collect stamps at Pepper's Pub, Black Moon Public House, Airline Brewing, and more. Drink specials and live music at each stop.

name: St. Patrick's Day Live Celtic Music at Black Moon
address: Black Moon Public House, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Special Irish menu all evening.
description: Black Moon Public House hosts a special evening of live Celtic music for St. Patrick's Day. Fiddle, bodhran, and tin whistle fill the pub while the kitchen serves an Irish-inspired menu. Green beer on tap.

name: Open Studio Night
description: Our regular midweek open studio session. Bring your own supplies or use what is available in the makerspace. Painting, drawing, printmaking, fiber arts, and more. A welcoming space for creative exploration.

name: Bargello Embroidery Workshop
note: Limited spots. Materials included.
description: Colleen Ross introduces bargello, the striking needlework technique known for its flame-like zigzag patterns. Learn to work on canvas with tapestry wool to create a small sampler piece. No prior needlework experience required.

name: Spring Equinox Film Night: Maine Outdoor Film Festival
note: Doors open at 6:30 PM. Suggested donation supports future screenings.
description: Celebrate the spring equinox with a curated selection of short films from the Maine Outdoor Film Festival. Stories of paddling, hiking, fishing, and living close to the land in Downeast Maine. Discussion follows the screening.

name: Live Music Saturday at Pepper's Pub
address: Pepper's Pub, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
description: Local singer-songwriter duo performs original folk and blues at Pepper's Pub. Full bar menu and pub fare available. A relaxed Saturday night out in downtown Ellsworth.

name: Dyed & Stab Bound Journal Workshop
note: All materials provided. Wear clothes that can get stained.
description: Karen Olson guides you through dyeing paper with natural plant dyes, then binding the sheets into a beautiful Japanese stab-bound journal. Learn about color from bark, berries, and roots while creating a one-of-a-kind handmade book.

name: Historic Walking Tour: Architecture of Main Street
address: Franklin Street Parklet, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Meet at the Franklin Street Parklet. About 1 mile, mostly flat.
description: Explore the architectural heritage of downtown Ellsworth on this guided walk. From Federal-era commercial buildings to early 20th century storefronts, discover how the built environment reflects the city's history as a Hancock County hub.

name: Coworking Day at the Makerspace
note: Wi-Fi, outlets, and coffee available. Bring your own lunch.
description: Need a change of scenery for your remote work? Heart of Ellsworth opens the makerspace as a free coworking space on Mondays. Desks, Wi-Fi, outlets, and fresh coffee provided. A quiet, creative environment to get things done.

name: Gelatin Printing: Abstract Landscapes
note: Ages 15+. Some prior gelatin printing experience helpful but not required.
description: Karin Otto leads an intermediate gelatin printing session focused on creating abstract landscape compositions. Explore torn-paper masks, color gradients, and multi-layer techniques to evoke the Downeast Maine scenery. All materials provided.

name: Open Studio Night
description: Drop into the studio for a creative evening. Work tables, tools, and basic supplies available. Bring your own project or start something new. A supportive space for painters, stitchers, printmakers, and makers of all kinds.

name: Community Meeting: Summer Programming Input Session
address: Ellsworth Public Library, 20 State Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Light refreshments provided. All community members welcome.
description: Heart of Ellsworth wants to hear from you. Share your ideas for summer workshops, concerts, markets, and events at this open input session held at the library. Help shape the 2026 summer calendar. Light refreshments served.

name: Downtown Pub Crawl: Art & Ales
address: Airline Brewing Co. Pub, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: 21+ event. Pick up your passport at Airline Brewing starting at 6 PM.
description: A self-guided crawl through downtown Ellsworth pubs, each hosting a local artist pop-up alongside drink specials. Stops include Airline Brewing, Finn's, Pepper's, Black Moon, and Zeppa's. Collect all passport stamps for a prize.

name: Live Music at Provender: Acoustic Evening
address: Provender Kitchen + Bar, 112 Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Reservations recommended for dinner.
description: Enjoy an acoustic performance from a Downeast singer-songwriter at Provender Kitchen + Bar. Seasonal cocktails, craft beer, and the full dinner menu available. A refined Friday evening out in downtown Ellsworth.

name: Kids Workshop: Paperbag Books & Journals
note: Ages 8+. Free admission; suggested $10 donation.
description: The popular paperbag bookmaking workshop returns. Kids fold, cut, and bind their own journals from recycled paper bags, then decorate the covers with stamps and collage. A fun, creative morning for young makers.

name: Stick & Stitch Embroidery: Spring Florals
note: Beginner-friendly. All materials included.
description: Colleen Ross teaches stick-and-stitch embroidery with a spring floral theme. Apply water-soluble patterns of wildflowers and ferns to fabric, then stitch over them with colorful thread. Perfect for beginners looking for a relaxing Saturday project.

name: Fundraising Dinner: Support the Makerspace
address: Provender Kitchen + Bar, 112 Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Tickets include 3-course dinner with wine pairings. Purchase at heartofellsworth.org or email cara@heartofellsworth.org.
description: Join Heart of Ellsworth for a special fundraising dinner at Provender Kitchen + Bar. Chef-prepared three-course meal with wine pairings, plus a silent auction featuring work by local artists. All proceeds support makerspace operations and free programming.

name: Open Studio: Sunday Drop-In
description: Spend a Sunday afternoon in the studio. Bring your own art supplies or use what is available. Easels, work tables, a printing press, and sewing machines are all accessible. A friendly community of makers to keep you company.

name: Coworking Day at the Makerspace
note: Wi-Fi, outlets, and coffee available.
description: Free coworking continues every Monday at Heart of Ellsworth. Desks, reliable Wi-Fi, power outlets, and hot coffee in a quiet creative environment. Open to remote workers, freelancers, and anyone needing a productive workspace.

name: Gelatin Printing: Monoprint Cards & Envelopes
note: Ages 15+. All materials provided. Take home a set of handmade cards.
description: Karin Otto shows you how to use the gelatin plate to create one-of-a-kind printed cards and matching envelopes. A practical and giftable project that lets you practice layering, masking, and color blending. All materials included.

name: Art Show: Downeast Light — Gallery Hours
note: Preview of the Downeast Light exhibition opening March 14.
description: Visit the 2nd floor gallery at Heart of Ellsworth to preview work being installed for the upcoming Downeast Light exhibition. Studio artists are on-site and happy to chat about their process and the show.

name: Wellness Wednesday: Gentle Yoga at the Library
address: Ellsworth Public Library, 20 State Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Bring your own mat or use one provided. All levels welcome.
description: A free lunchtime yoga session in the library community room, offered in partnership with Heart of Ellsworth. Gentle flow and breathwork suitable for all levels. A midweek reset for body and mind.

name: Zeppa's Trivia Night: Ellsworth Edition
address: Zeppa's NY Pizza, Main Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Teams of up to 6. Prizes for top 3 teams.
description: Test your knowledge of Ellsworth history, Maine trivia, and pop culture at this lively pub trivia night hosted at Zeppa's. Form a team of up to six and compete for gift card prizes. Pizza and drinks available for purchase.

name: Young Makers Holiday Craft Workshop
note: Led by Ellsworth NHS student instructors. All ages welcome.
description: Ellsworth High School National Honor Society students lead a free craft workshop for kids and families. Projects include spring-themed crafts using recycled materials. A great opportunity for young makers to learn from teen mentors.

name: Wellness Wednesday: Gentle Yoga at the Library
address: Ellsworth Public Library, 20 State Street, Ellsworth, ME 04605
note: Bring your own mat or use one provided.
description: Midweek lunchtime yoga continues at the library. A gentle, accessible flow led by a local instructor in the community room. Free and open to all experience levels. Mats available if you do not have your own.

name: Art Show: Downeast Light — Gallery Hours
description: The Downeast Light group exhibition continues with Saturday gallery hours at Heart of Ellsworth. Paintings, photography, and prints by twelve local artists exploring Maine coastal light. Free and open to all.

name: Art Show: Downeast Light — Gallery Hours
note: Last weekend to see the exhibition.
description: Final Saturday gallery hours for the Downeast Light group exhibition. Last chance to see work by twelve Hancock County artists before the show comes down. Several pieces available for purchase.

name: Closing Reception: Downeast Light Exhibition
note: Last chance to see the show. Wine and cheese served.
description: Join the artists of the Downeast Light exhibition for a closing reception with wine, cheese, and conversation. Meet the twelve participating artists, hear about their work, and purchase pieces before the show comes down.
